title: Use purged sequential validation and embargoed OOF encodings as model-selection guardrails
summary: Because the label asks about the next lap, adjacent rows within a driver stint have overlapping information.
rationale: Because the label asks about the next lap, adjacent rows within a driver stint have overlapping information. Standard grouped CV can still overstate performance if train rows sit immediately before/after test rows in the same chronological neighborhood.
implementation_hint: Evaluate candidates with race-driver sequential splits that purge nearby laps around each validation block and build every target/frequency/group statistic strictly OOF. Compare this against current CV before trusting tiny gains above 0.9511.
expected_effect: May not raise local CV directly, but should improve leaderboard transfer and help reject features that only exploit overlap leakage.
risk: Stricter CV can lower apparent score and slow search; useful mainly as a filter for deciding which ideas are real.
